If it ain’t rice then it ain’t nice

blueprints.setup_instruction: Boy opens with a totally innocent, almost absurd question ('do you like water?', 'can you hold your breath?', 'how do you feel about ice?'). Must sound completely benign — no trace of innuendo.
blueprints.payoff_instruction: After girl's confused reaction (yes?? / why? / what does that mean?), boy lands a numeric or double-meaning punchline that reframes the setup as spicy (e.g., 'because you're like 70% my type', 'because you're about to be soaked in compliments', 'good because I need someone who can handle a little heat').

example_exchanges.boy2: because you're one cool move away from melting me

blueprints.comment_bait_hint: The '70% my type' or numeric payoff is inherently screenshot-bait. Set up so viewers can share the punchline line alone.
blueprints.screenshot_hint: The boy's payoff line should be short and quotable (under 10 words). It should work as a standalone text.
blueprints.safety_note: PG-13 innuendo only. No explicit sexual content. The double meaning should be implied, not stated.

blueprints.setup_instruction: Boy asks an innocent but slightly unusual physical question about a body part or action ('can I use your thighs as earmuffs?', 'is your shoulder a good pillow?', 'how warm are your hands?'). The question sounds weird but not overtly sexual.
blueprints.payoff_instruction: Girl hesitates or responds with confusion/thinking (hmm... / hm. / ok wait). Boy then doubles down with a short, confident declaration that makes the innuendo crystal clear but still non-explicit ('don't play... you know that's exactly where my face belongs').

example_exchanges.boy: can I use your thighs as earmuffs??
example_exchanges.boy2: don't play... you know that's exactly where my face belongs

example_exchanges.boy: is your neck a good pillow?
example_exchanges.boy2: i just like things soft and close

blueprints.comment_bait_hint: The buildup through multiple girl 'hmm' responses creates tension. Viewers debate if she'll say yes.
blueprints.screenshot_hint: Boy's final declaration should be bold and short — the contrast between sweet setup and this line is the screenshot moment.
blueprints.safety_note: Implication must stay PG-13. The joke is about closeness/warmth, not explicit acts.

blueprints.setup_instruction: Boy opens with a bold but seemingly innocent claim ('I know 70 ways to make you happy', 'I have 100 reasons to text you back'). This sets up curiosity — girl wants to know more.
blueprints.payoff_instruction: Girl asks for the first one or says 'and the rest?'. Boy reveals a sweet/innocent first item, then when pushed for more, the final answer is a number-based innuendo (e.g., 'the rest is 69', 'the last one starts with a 6 and ends with a 9').

example_exchanges.boy: I know 70 ways to make you happy

blueprints.comment_bait_hint: The '69' reveal is pure comment-bait — viewers will flood comments with laughing emojis and 'he said it'.
blueprints.screenshot_hint: The exchange 'I know 70 ways to make you happy → the rest is 69' is the screenshot. Keep boy's answer short.
blueprints.safety_note: 69 is a well-known innuendo that stays safe for most platforms. Do not go more explicit.

blueprints.setup_instruction: Boy sends a completely random, left-field question mid-conversation ('quick question. favorite color?', 'real quick — do you believe in aliens?', 'random but: can you cook?'). The question feels disconnected from anything that came before.
blueprints.payoff_instruction: Girl responds confused or curious ('why?' / 'what does that have to do with anything?'). Boy then lands a punchline that connects the random question back to a flirty intent ('just checking if we're compatible before I ruin your week', 'aliens because I've never seen anything like you', 'because I want to know if I should bring groceries or a reservation').

example_exchanges.boy2: just checking if we're compatible before i ruin your week

example_exchanges.boy: real quick — do you believe in aliens?
example_exchanges.boy2: because i've never seen anything like you in my section of the universe

blueprints.comment_bait_hint: The random pivot creates a 'wait what?' moment that makes viewers rewatch. The payoff lands harder because of the setup.
blueprints.screenshot_hint: The punchline after the pivot is the screenshot moment. Make it surprising and quotable.
blueprints.safety_note: Keep it witty and playful. No explicit content. The humor is in the unexpected connection.

blueprints.setup_instruction: Boy acts as if they're already dating or already have a plan ('I already told my mom about us', 'what time should I pick you up', 'I already planned our first argument'). This is presumptuous and slightly chaotic — she never agreed to anything.
blueprints.payoff_instruction: Girl pushes back with confusion or skepticism ('you don't know me' / '???' / 'who said that?'). Boy responds with either a callback that reframes his presumption as confidence ('acknowledgment's a formality, interest speaks louder') or a funny escalation.

example_exchanges.boy: I already told my mom about us
example_exchanges.girl: she should have warned me before i replied tbh
example_exchanges.boy2: she said you would say that

example_exchanges.boy: I would never play hide and seek with you
example_exchanges.boy2: because someone like you is impossible to hide from

blueprints.comment_bait_hint: The chaos of acting like they're already together drives comments like 'the confidence' and 'he said MOM'.
blueprints.screenshot_hint: The original bold claim + her confused reaction is the screenshot. Keep the boy's opener under 10 words.
blueprints.safety_note: Confident not coercive. The humor is in the presumption, not pressure.

blueprints.setup_instruction: Boy sends an innocent-sounding compliment or observation ('your body is tea', 'you're giving tea energy', 'you look like a green flag'). She takes it literally or responds warmly.
blueprints.payoff_instruction: After her warm/neutral response, boy escalates with a second message that adds the innuendo layer ('I meant I want to drink you in slowly', 'hot and worth every sip', 'and I drink a lot of tea'). The payoff reframes the compliment.

example_exchanges.boy2: hot. worth every sip. keeping you.
the kind you want to take your time with

blueprints.comment_bait_hint: The flip from 'innocent compliment' to spicy second layer is the comment-bait — viewers love the double-take.
blueprints.screenshot_hint: The pair of messages ('your body is tea' + the follow-up) creates a quotable exchange.
blueprints.safety_note: Tea = attractive, not explicit. Keep the innuendo about warmth and desirability, not body parts.

blueprints.setup_instruction: Boy opens with a rating or scoring mechanic ('you're the first 10/10 I've ever seen', 'I'd give you a solid 8/10'). This provokes either pride or pushback.
blueprints.payoff_instruction: When girl reacts, boy backtracks slightly ('yeah my bad, you're more like 9/10') which makes her defensive. Then when she pushes back asking what took points off, boy lands a clever or spicy answer that implies what the missing point could buy.

example_exchanges.boy: You the first 10/10 i ever seen
example_exchanges.boy2: yeah yu right you more like 9/10
example_exchanges.girl2: So what made it go down?

blueprints.comment_bait_hint: Dropping her rating on purpose makes viewers want to know what the answer is — they comment guessing.
blueprints.screenshot_hint: The 'you the first 10/10' line is screenshot-worthy if paired with the rating drop.
blueprints.safety_note: The missing point implies attraction, not a physical flaw. Keep it flirtatious not mean.
